trendingTopics.name: Alternate History Scenarios
trendingTopics.targetAudience: History enthusiasts, alternate reality fiction fans, curious general audience
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if the dinosaurs never went extinct and evolved intelligence?

trendingTopics.name: Everyday Physics/Biology Changes
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Science fiction fans, curious individuals, students
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if gravity suddenly reversed for 5 minutes globally?

trendingTopics.name: Fictional Universe Crossovers/Twists
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Pop culture fans, specific fandoms (Marvel, Star Wars, Anime, Gaming)
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if Harry Potter was sorted into Slytherin and befriended Voldemort?

trendingTopics.name: Technological Advancements/Regressions
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Tech enthusiasts, futurists, general audience interested in sci-fi
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if AI gained full sentience and took over all internet communication?

trendingTopics.name: Philosophical Concepts Literalized
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if 'ignorance is bliss' became a literal superpower you could activate?

viralFormats: Rapid-fire narration with bold text overlays and dynamic visuals
Short animated sequences depicting the 'what if' scenario
POV acting out an immediate consequence of the hypothetical
Comparison charts or split screens showing 'before vs. after' the hypothetical
hooks: This 'what if' will blow your mind...
The craziest 'what if' question you'll hear today...
videoStructures: Hook -> Introduce hypothetical -> Brief visual/audio exploration of scenario -> Implied consequence/open question
Hook -> State 'what if' -> Act out a short, immediate reaction/outcome -> Punchline/cliffhanger
Hook -> Present 'normal' situation -> Twist with 'what if' -> Rapid-fire visual examples of new reality -> Call to action to comment
popularMusic: Fast-paced, high-energy background music for quick cuts
Sound effects for emphasis (whooshes, dings, dramatic stings)

trendingTopics.name: Alternate History Scenarios
trendingTopics.targetAudience: History enthusiasts, casual learners, alternate history fiction readers (Gen Z & Millennials)
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if the Roman Empire never fell, leading to a technologically advanced, pan-European empire in the modern age?

trendingTopics.name: Hypothetical Scientific/Physical Changes
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Science buffs, curious minds, students interested in physics/astronomy (Teens to Young Adults)
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if Earth had two suns, drastically altering climate, life cycles, and human society?

trendingTopics.name: Everyday Life with Extreme Rules
trendingTopics.targetAudience: General curious audience, Gen Z looking for thought experiments and relatable absurdism
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if every time you said 'hello', a random item from your house disappeared permanently?

trendingTopics.name: Fictional Crossover Scenarios
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Pop culture fans, specific fandoms (e.g., Marvel, Anime, Gaming communities)
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if characters from 'The Office' were suddenly transported into the 'Game of Thrones' universe?

trendingTopics.name: Future Technology/Societal Impact
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Tech enthusiasts, futurists, ethical AI debaters (Millennials & Gen Z)
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if brain-computer interfaces became mandatory for all education, making traditional learning obsolete?

viralFormats: Text-on-screen with dynamic visuals and rapid cuts
Green screen explanations with creator reacting or illustrating points
Animated explainers (simple motion graphics or whiteboard style)
POV scenarios with voiceover and on-screen text
Comparison/Contrast visuals (e.g., 'our world vs. what if world')
hooks: The one change that would alter EVERYTHING.
You won't believe what happens if...
videoStructures: Hook (Question) -> Setup of Premise -> Immediate Consequences -> Long-term Societal/Environmental Shifts -> Call to Action/Cliffhanger
Question -> Brief Background -> Key Implication 1 -> Key Implication 2 -> Quick Summary/Open-ended Question
Problem (the 'what if') -> Exploration of cause-and-effect -> Visual demonstration -> Concluding thought
popularMusic: Suspenseful instrumental background scores (e.g., 'Interstellar' style ambient)
Trending TikTok/Shorts sounds that evoke curiosity or mild tension
Mysterious lo-fi or 'dark academia' type beats
Dramatic sound effects (whooshes, dings, impact sounds)

trendingTopics.name: Historical Reversal What Ifs
trendingTopics.targetAudience: History buffs, alternate history enthusiasts, strategic thinkers
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if the Roman Empire never fell and continued to exist into the modern era?

trendingTopics.name: Science Fiction & Fantasy What Ifs
trendingTopics.targetAudience: Sci-fi fans, fantasy enthusiasts, speculative fiction readers, AI art fans
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if magic suddenly became real and everyone had a unique, random spell they could cast once?

trendingTopics.name: Everyday Life & Societal What Ifs
trendingTopics.targetAudience: General audience, philosophical thinkers, those seeking relatable hypotheticals
trendingTopics.exampleIdea: What if money ceased to exist overnight and society had to revert to a pure bartering system?

viralFormats: Fast-paced narration with AI-generated or stock footage visuals
Text-on-screen with dramatic music and quick cuts
Animated short sequences illustrating the 'what if' outcome
Comparison shorts: 'Reality vs. What If' with split screens
hooks: What if you woke up tomorrow and...?
Imagine a world where one small change...?
This one historical event could have changed EVERYTHING.
The consequences of X would be unimaginable.
You think you know how X works? What if it didn't?
videoStructures: Hook (question/statement) -> Introduce scenario -> Explore 2-3 key impacts/changes -> Concluding thought/question for audience.
Dramatic opening visual/music -> 'What if' premise -> Rapid montage of potential outcomes -> Call to action (comment).
Problem (current/historical) -> 'What if' a key variable changed -> Visualize the new reality.
popularMusic: Dramatic cinematic scores (e.g., 'Two Steps From Hell' style)
Upbeat, whimsical tunes for lighter 'what if' scenarios
